findings.title: NaijaS2ST: A Multi-Accent Benchmark for Speech-to-Speech Translation in Low-Resource Nigerian Languages
findings.abstract: Speech translation for low-resource languages remains fundamentally limited by the scarcity of high-quality, diverse parallel speech data, a challenge that is especially pronounced in African linguistic contexts. To address this, we introduce NaijaS2ST, a parallel speech translation dataset spanning

findings.title: ASMR-Bench: Auditing for Sabotage in ML Research
findings.abstract: As AI systems are increasingly used to conduct research autonomously, misaligned systems could introduce subtle flaws that produce misleading results while evading detection. We introduce ASMR-Bench (Auditing for Sabotage in ML Research), a benchmark for evaluating the ability of auditors to detect

findings.title: Using Large Language Models and Knowledge Graphs to Improve the Interpretability of Machine Learning Models in Manufacturing
findings.abstract: Explaining Machine Learning (ML) results in a transparent and user-friendly manner remains a challenging task of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI). findings.title: Evaluating the Progression of Large Language Model Capabilities for Small-Molecule Drug Design
findings.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have the potential to accelerate small molecule drug design due to their ability to reason about information from diverse sources and formats. findings.title: Learning to Reason with Insight for Informal Theorem Proving
findings.abstract: Although most of the automated theorem-proving approaches depend on formal proof systems, informal theorem proving can align better with large language models' (LLMs) strength in natural language processing. findings.title: No Universal Courtesy: A Cross-Linguistic, Multi-Model Study of Politeness Effects on LLMs Using the PLUM Corpus
findings.abstract: This paper explores the response of Large Language Models (LLMs) to user prompts with different degrees of politeness and impoliteness. findings.title: From Benchmarking to Reasoning: A Dual-Aspect, Large-Scale Evaluation of LLMs on Vietnamese Legal Text
findings.abstract: The complexity of Vietnam's legal texts presents a significant barrier to public access to justice. findings.title: Semantic Area Graph Reasoning for Multi-Robot Language-Guided Search
findings.abstract: Coordinating multi-robot systems (MRS) to search in unknown environments is particularly challenging for tasks that require semantic reasoning beyond geometric exploration. findings.title: SwanNLP at SemEval-2026 Task 5: An LLM-based Framework for Plausibility Scoring in Narrative Word Sense Disambiguation
findings.abstract: Recent advances in language models have substantially improved Natural Language Understanding (NLU). Although widely used benchmarks suggest that Large Language Models (LLMs) can effectively disambiguate, their practical applicability in real-world narrative contexts remains underexplored. findings.title: Characterising LLM-Generated Competency Questions: a Cross-Domain Empirical Study using Open and Closed Models
findings.abstract: Competency Questions (CQs) are a cornerstone of requirement elicitation in ontology engineering. findings.title: Do Vision-Language Models Truly Perform Vision Reasoning? A Rigorous Study of the Modality Gap
findings.abstract: Reasoning in vision-language models (VLMs) has recently attracted significant attention due to its broad applicability across diverse downstream tasks. However, it remains unclear whether the superior performance of VLMs stems from genuine vision-grounded reasoning or relies predominantly on the rea

findings.title: Detecting and Suppressing Reward Hacking with Gradient Fingerprints
findings.abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) typically optimizes for outcome rewards without imposing constraints on intermediate reasoning. findings.title: Optimizing Korean-Centric LLMs via Token Pruning
findings.abstract: This paper presents a systematic benchmark of state-of-the-art multilingual large language models (LLMs) adapted via token pruning - a compression technique that eliminates tokens and embedding parameters corresponding to languages irrelevant to the target application. findings.title: A Two-Stage, Object-Centric Deep Learning Framework for Robust Exam Cheating Detection
findings.abstract: Academic integrity continues to face the persistent challenge of examination cheating. Traditional invigilation relies on human observation, which is inefficient, costly, and prone to errors at scale. findings.title: Beyond Surface Statistics: Robust Conformal Prediction for LLMs via Internal Representations
findings.abstract: Large language models are increasingly deployed in settings where reliability matters, yet output-level uncertainty signals such as token probabilities, entropy, and self-consistency can become brittle under calibration--deployment mismatch. findings.title: NVBench: A Benchmark for Speech Synthesis with Non-Verbal Vocalizations
findings.abstract: Non-verbal vocalizations (NVVs) like laugh, sigh, and sob are essential for human-like speech, yet standardized evaluation remains limited in jointly assessing whether systems can generate the intended NVVs, place them correctly, and keep them salient without harming speech. findings.title: ChemGraph-XANES: An Agentic Framework for XANES Simulation and Analysis
findings.abstract: Computational X-ray absorption near-edge structure (XANES) is widely used to probe local coordination environments, oxidation states, and electronic structure in chemically complex systems. findings.title: Bridging the Gap between User Intent and LLM: A Requirement Alignment Approach for Code Generation
findings.abstract: Code generation refers to automatically producing executable programs from user requirements. findings.title: Sketching the Readout of Large Language Models for Scalable Data Attribution and Valuation

findings.title: Empirical Asymptotic Runtime Analysis of Linear Programming Algorithms
findings.abstract: This paper takes an empirical look at asymptotic runtime growth rates for the most widely used algorithms for solving linear programming (LP) problems across a set of six optimization application areas that are known to produce large and difficult LP models.
